name: Letterboxd Mirror

releases.author: diego-vicente
releases.description:
## Eta Templating Engine

This release introduces [Eta](https://eta.js.org/) as the templating engine, providing a powerful and flexible templating experience.

### New Features

- **Fluent API**: Chain methods for formatting — `it.cast.top(5).link().bullet()`
- **Full JavaScript support**: Use any JS expression in templates
- **Rating methods**: `it.userRating.stars()`, `it.userRating.over(10)`
- **Image sizing**: `it.poster.size("L")` or `it.poster.size(500)`
- **Cast with roles**: `it.castWithRoles.top(5).linkActors().bullet()`
- **YAML helpers**: `.yaml()` for inline arrays, `.yamlMultiline()` for lists

### Template Syntax

Templates use Eta syntax with the `it.` prefix for all variables:

```eta
<%= it.filmTitle %>                    Output a value
<%= it.genres.link().bullet() %>       Chain methods
<% if (it.rewatch.isTrue()) { %>       JavaScript conditionals
  (rewatch)
<% } %>
```

### Documentation

- Comprehensive [Template Variables](docs/Template-Variables.md) reference
- New [Usage Guide](docs/Usage.md) and [Installation Guide](docs/Installation.md)
- Recipes section with copy-paste examples

releases.author: diego-vicente
releases.description:
## What's New

### RSS sync now fetches tags

Previously, tags were only available via CSV import. Now RSS sync fetches tags directly from Letterboxd, so you get complete diary entries without needing a CSV import.

### Simplified sync logic

Both RSS and CSV import now use the same simple logic: if a note with the same viewing ID exists, skip it; otherwise, create a new note. Notes are never modified after creation.

releases.author: diego-vicente
releases.description:
## Changes

Addressed all warnings from the Obsidian plugin review bot.

### Linting Improvements

- Added `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` with recommended rules to catch Obsidian-specific issues
- Upgraded to ESLint v9 with flat config format
- CI now automatically catches these issues on every PR

### Code Quality Fixes

- **Sentence case in UI text**: Fixed 15 occurrences to follow Obsidian guidelines
- **Floating promises**: Added `void` operator to explicitly ignore promises in callbacks
- **Async/await**: Removed unnecessary `async` from functions that don't use `await`
- **Test isolation**: Excluded e2e/ from plugin linting (uses Node.js APIs for testing only)

releases.author: diego-vicente
releases.description:
## Changes

- Fixed plugin description

## Code Quality Improvements

Improved compliance with Obsidian plugin guidelines:

- **Removed unnecessary console logging**: Cleaned up debug logs from CSV parsing, file discovery, and template engine
- **Added `normalizePath()`**: User-defined folder paths are now properly normalized for cross-platform compatibility
- **Used `Vault.process()` for atomic updates**: CSV import now uses `Vault.process()` instead of `Vault.modify()` for safer file modifications
- **Extracted shared `notify()` utility**: Consolidated duplicate notification helper into `src/utils/notify.ts`
- **Fixed template docs URL**: Template editor now links to the correct GitHub wiki documentation

releases.author: diego-vicente
releases.description:
Initial release of Letterboxd Mirror.

## Features

- **RSS sync**: Automatically fetches your diary entries from Letterboxd's RSS feed.
- **CSV import**: Import your complete Letterboxd data export for tags and historical entries.
- **TMDB integration**: Create rich Film notes with cast, crew, and metadata from The Movie Database.
- **Rewatch support**: Each viewing gets its own note with a unique ID.
- **Customizable templates**: Full control over filenames and note content.
- **Auto-sync on startup**: Optionally sync new entries when Obsidian launches.
- **Deduplication**: Automatically skips entries that already exist in your vault.
